title: Drive the real single-device execute path instead of waiting on passive evidence

stories.title: Connect one bounded monitor/log onboarding path to a real controller-backed execution result
stories.scope: Take one exact single-device continue-onboarding monitor/log path that is still stopping at local-only contract evidence, and make the backend/frontend contract carry a real controller-backed execution result or an exact stage-specific blocker instead of a passive local-only summary. Keep the change bounded to one execution path and preserve honest non-success outcomes.
nonGoals: Do not reopen browser-only validation as the main task.
Do not fabricate remote success when the controller or remote step did not actually run.
acceptance: One bounded single-device monitor/log onboarding path now reports a real controller-backed execution result or an exact stage-specific blocker, not just local evidence persistence.
The resulting onboarding action evidence preserves success, failed, blocked, retryable, or unknown outcomes honestly for that path.
Focused backend or frontend validation covers the changed execution path.
notes: This story exists because batch-005 completed without landing real single-device execute evidence, so the next batch must resume implementation rather than waiting passively.

stories.title: Run one real single-device continue-onboarding execution and capture durable monitor/log evidence
stories.scope: Use the repaired bounded continue-onboarding path to run one real single-device execution attempt and capture durable evidence of the actual monitor/log result or the exact concrete blocker returned by the controller-backed path. Keep the evidence explicit about what truly executed and what remained blocked.
nonGoals: Do not perform batch onboarding execution.
Do not downgrade a real execution blocker into local-only success wording.
Do not widen scope beyond one bounded single-device execution attempt.
acceptance: A durable evidence summary is written for one real single-device execution attempt.
The evidence states whether the path reached a controller-backed monitor/log execution result or stopped at an exact blocker.
Any non-success, retryable, blocked, or unknown outcome from that execution attempt is preserved explicitly.
notes: If the real execution still cannot proceed, the blocker must come from the controller-backed path itself, not from passive local-only evidence handling.

stories.title: Carry the real execution result into readiness and decide whether another execution batch is needed
stories.scope: Update the durable readiness and review artifacts so the next PRD handoff is driven by the new real execution result instead of the old passive-wait conclusion. If one final honesty mismatch remains inside the allowed paths, fix it before closing the story; otherwise record the exact next execution or PRD boundary decision.
nonGoals: Do not close the whole program unless the new execution result really justifies it.
Do not reintroduce browser-only closure language.
acceptance: final-readiness.md and review.md reflect the new real execution result rather than passive waiting on batch-005.
If a concrete frontend/backend honesty mismatch remains inside allowed paths, it is fixed and validated before the readiness update is finalized.
The next-step note clearly says whether another execution batch is needed or whether PRD scope should change.
notes: This story is the execution-side close-out for batch-006 and should remove the current passive-wait drift between d2on-prd and d2on.
